name: SUID Runner Misconfiguration (Medium)
story: A maintenance team deployed a small SUID helper to run system health checks with root privileges. The binary itself looks simple, but it delegates the real work to a shell script stored in a maintenance directory. That script is writable by the maintenance group, which includes the low-privileged user. Your task is to connect those facts, modify the privileged execution path, and use the helper to make root-only data readable.
objectives: Discover writable privileged script execution and exploit it to read /root/flag.txt.

steps.title: Discover the privileged helper
steps.description: Enumerate SUID binaries and identify the custom maintenance runner.
steps.question: What is the path of the vulnerable SUID binary?

hints.text: Look for a non-standard binary under /usr/local/bin.

steps.title: Find writable privileged execution
steps.description: Identify which script is executed by the SUID binary and verify write access as student.
steps.question: Which script file can be edited and is executed by the SUID helper?

hints.text: Inspect strings output of the SUID binary.
Check ownership and group permissions of /opt/maint/run-check.sh.

steps.title: Exploit and capture flag
steps.description: Inject a payload in the writable script to copy the flag to a readable location, then run the SUID helper.
steps.question: What is the captured flag?

hints.text: Append a command that copies /root/flag.txt into /tmp/flag.txt.
